metadata.title:
Ask COUNCIL
To Make new


ORDINANCE

pages.raw_text:
The council chamber was throng-
ed last night with a crowd eager
to hear the discussion of the street
speaking ordinance. There were two
pleas for the passage of new or
dinance and the repeal of the old
one, but no discussion of the ques-
ton by the city council occurred.
1t was voted to meet at o'clock
this afternoon in committee of the
whole to consider the matter.


r. j. Tamblen, representing the
socialists of Spokane, appeared with
a petition asking for the revocation
of the present ordinance and the
passage of one in its stead per-
hitting street speaking under po-
lice supervision.


Carl Evans, of the typographical
union, representing the committee
of five members appointed by the
Central Labor council at its meet
ing Monday night, said that he had
no sympathy for the Industrial
Workers as an organization but was
with them in their efforts to secure
their rights of free speech granted
by the constitution He pointed to
the speeches of Taft and Bryan as
violations of the street speaking or
dinance that were permitted with
out objection by the police, and
cited this as discrimination.


A committee of three councilmen
was appointed to investigate condi.
tons at the city Jail. This commit
tee consists of Councilmen Nelson
Mohr and Shaefer, who will report
their findings this afternoon.
